data.title: Pubs Cheer Business Rates Relief While Other Firms Left Out in the Cold
data.description:
Andy Burnham’s 20% business rates discount for pubs, social clubs and live music venues was welcomed by hospitality bosses, with tax experts saying it could save the average pub more than £1,000, according to BBC. The relief comes on top of emergency business rates support introduced in January.

But other High Street operators said they would miss out and urged ministers to widen the package. Keith Bott, who runs the Titanic Brewery and nine pubs across the Midlands, said pubs face pressure from higher National Insurance, wage costs, inflation and living costs, while Ruth Dawson of HD3 Fitness Centre in Huddersfield said her gym had been “left out in the cold”. Nick Smith, who runs the Ludoquist board game cafe and bar in Croydon, said he feared being excluded because his venue may not meet the government’s definition of a pub. Sam Lamiroy, who runs 45 Queen Street in Penzance, said a cut in VAT from 20% to 10% would be more effective than the business rates relief.

data.title: Jim Cramer Warns Wall Street Is Underestimating Oil and AI Spending Risks
data.description:
According to CNBC, Jim Cramer said Wall Street may be underestimating a growing set of risks for stocks, including higher oil prices and heavier spending on artificial intelligence. Speaking on "Squawk on the Street" before Thursday's market open, he said he was struggling to find reasons to buy and had plenty of reasons to sell.

He pointed to a weak session in which the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell more than 400 points, the S&P 500 dropped more than 1%, and the Nasdaq Composite lost about 2%. Cramer also said the S&P 500 was down just 1.5% from its June 2 record close as of Wednesday's finish, while the Nasdaq was about 5% below its June 2 peak and the Dow was about 1.6% under its July 6 record close.

Cramer said Brent crude surged more than 6% to above $100 a barrel after Houthi militants in Yemen said they attacked two Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea, and after President Donald Trump warned the U.S. would retaliate and bomb Iranian infrastructure. He said the move in crude lifted inflation concerns and pushed the 10-year Treasury yield to its highest level since January. The Federal Reserve's policy committee is due to meet next week, and CME FedWatch put the odds of a quarter-point rate increase at 38%, up from 12% a week earlier.

He also said Alphabet's latest quarter highlighted the cost of the AI buildout. Alphabet recently said it planned to raise roughly $85 billion through stock sales, on top of higher debt issuance this year.

data.title: AMD Expects Global Data Center CPU Market to Reach $220 Billion by 2030
data.description: According to Jin10, AMD said the global data center CPU market is expected to reach $220 billion by 2030.

data.title: Pantheon Electric Eyes Copper Acquisitions for Data Center Demand
data.description: Pantheon Electric plans to expand through acquisitions to meet rising demand from artificial intelligence data centers and grid upgrades, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Goldman Sachs CEO Urges Passage of Crypto CLARITY Act as Senate Republicans Release New Draft
data.description:
Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon urged U.S. regulators to pass the Crypto CLARITY Act to establish a market structure for digital assets. According to NS3.AI, JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon said the bill could put traditional banks at a disadvantage.

U.S. Senate Republicans released a new draft on Wednesday for discussion.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Microsoft Replaces OpenAI Image Model With Its Own in PowerPoint and Bing
data.description: Microsoft (MSFT.O) is using its own image generation model instead of OpenAI's image model in PowerPoint and Bing. According to Jin10, the change applies to PowerPoint and Bing.

data.title: Nikkei Asia: Hidden AI Debt at Five U.S. Tech Giants Reaches $1.65 Trillion
data.description: Nikkei Asia reported on July 21 that the hidden debt tied to opaque AI financing at five U.S. technology giants has surged to $1.65 trillion, according to Jiemian News. After reviewing the financial reports and other materials of Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta and Oracle, the publication said their off-balance-sheet debt had increased eightfold over the past four years and reached $1.65 trillion in the latest quarter, far above their roughly $1.35 trillion in on-balance-sheet liabilities.

data.title: Market Sentiment Turns Cautious As India Protest Searches Surge 1,350%
data.description: Public data found no evidence of a broad crypto-funding wave during India’s Cockroach Janta Party protest escalation, even as protest searches rose about 1,350% from July 18 to July 22. According to NS3.AI, crypto-buying searches fell about 18%, estimated USDT/INR turnover dropped about 30%, and the USDT premium narrowed by roughly 44%. Four unofficial Solana tokens generated about $1.48 million in decentralized exchange volume, but there was no public evidence linking them to protest organisers.

data.title: STOCKS | Micron Trades 24% Below June High as Analysts Debate Recovery
data.description: Micron Technology traded at $959.48, about 24% below its June all-time high of $1,255, as analysts discussed whether the recent rally can resume. According to NS3.AI, Wall Street’s average price target is near $1,300, while bear-case models range from $539 to $740.

data.title: Seattle Seahawks Owners Eye Mark Stevens, Private Equity Investors
data.description: The new owners of the NFL's Seattle Seahawks, led by Vinod and Neeru Khosla, are in talks with multiple parties to join their consortium, according to Bloomberg. The potential investors include Mark Stevens and private equity firms, people familiar with the matter said.

data.title: Microsoft To Test Ad-Supported Xbox Cloud Gaming, Amazon Adds Luna To Prime Video
data.description: According to CNBC, Microsoft’s Xbox division said Thursday it will test an advertising-supported way for users to stream video games, while Amazon said it will add its Luna cloud gaming service to Prime Video. Amazon’s Prime subscription costs $14.99 per month, and the move will give Luna more prominent placement on its website after it was previously available only through a dedicated site. The companies have both been successful in cloud computing but have struggled to get users playing games over sometimes unreliable internet connections that can create latency. Xbox said members of the Xbox Insider Program can join the test with a one-hour session limit, and it applies to games already in a user’s library. Amazon gaming head Jeff Gattis said the company’s goal is to reach more casual players, that Luna has “millions” of users across the U.S. and 13 other countries, and that it aims to reach 10 million to 20 million users as quickly as possible.

data.title: BlackRock's GIP Eyes Securitization Deal for Fund Stakes
data.description:
BlackRock Inc.'s Global Infrastructure Partners LP has been in talks with investors to sell bonds backed by stakes in some of its private capital funds, Bloomberg reported.

The move would put GIP among a growing list of asset managers exploring securitization as a way to boost returns on their holdings.

data.title: Honda Confirms Next-Generation Ridgeline Will Be Produced in the U.S.
data.description: According to CNBC, Honda Motor confirmed that a next-generation Ridgeline pickup truck will be produced in the U.S. after a temporary production stoppage later this year. The Alabama plant that assembles the midsize truck will halt production later this year and is expected to resume building the vehicle within two years, likely in 2028. Lance Woelfer, vice president of auto sales at American Honda Motor, said the company wants to keep serving loyal Ridgeline customers while adding more ruggedness and capability to the truck. He declined to say whether the next version will use a body-on-frame structure. Honda also said the future Ridgeline model was teased in an image released on July 23, 2026. Ridgeline sales fell about 3% in the first half of the year, and Honda has sold roughly 41,000 to 52,000 of the trucks annually since 2021, versus more than 270,000 Toyota Tacoma units sold in 2025.

data.title: AI TRENDS | xAI Releases Grok 4.5 on iOS, Android, Web, and X
data.description: xAI announced the release of Grok 4.5 on iOS, Android, the web, and X. According to Odaily, the rollout covers all four platforms.

data.title: Multicoin Capital Co-Founder Rejects Proposal for Advance Trade Disclosure
data.description:
Multicoin Capital co-founder Tushar Jain said it is "completely absurd" to ask traders to disclose their trading intentions in advance, responding to comments from Nansen co-founder Alex Svanevik. He said no trader would want to reveal intentions ahead of time and face front-running, and that no trader would want their liquidation prices made public.

According to Foresight News, Jain said privacy is crucial to the successful operation of onchain capital markets. He also said a large batch of HYPE tokens he previously unstaked was not intended for sale, but that he was forced to regularly change wallets because the funds were continuously being tracked.

Svanevik had said transparency is a feature rather than a flaw and suggested that if funds are already being tracked, it would be better to disclose intentions in advance. Jain made the remarks in response to that view.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports Explosion in Kuwait
data.description: According to Jin10, Iranian Mehr News Agency reported an explosion in Kuwait.

data.title: Drone Navigation Firms Develop Jamming-Resistant Systems
data.description: GPS spoofing and other battlefield countermeasures are prompting drone navigation companies to develop systems that can operate despite intense jamming, according to Bloomberg. The new approaches are designed to keep drones flying even when satellite signals are disrupted over war zones.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia Eyes Longer Diesel Export Ban as Ukraine Hits Oil Assets
data.description: Russia is considering extending its diesel-export ban for another month as Ukraine intensifies attacks on the country’s oil industry, Interfax reported according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | APEC Digital and AI Meeting Elevates Open Source to Ministerial Cooperation Level
data.description:
The 2026 APEC Digital and Artificial Intelligence Ministers' Meeting press conference was held in Chengdu on the 23rd. According to Foresight News, Minister of Industry and Information Technology Li Lecheng said the meeting's outcomes included elevating open source to the APEC ministerial cooperation level for the first time.

Li said the meeting emphasized the importance of respecting intellectual property rights while encouraging member economies to support open source practices and take part in international open source collaboration in artificial intelligence.

data.title: Africa Finance Corp., Afreximbank Back Dangote Refinery’s $2.5 Billion Placement
data.description:
Africa Finance Corp. and Africa Export-Import Bank were among investors in Dangote Petroleum Refinery & Petrochemicals FZE’s $2.5 billion private share placement as the Nigerian company prepares for what would be Africa’s biggest initial public offering, according to Bloomberg.

The fundraising adds support for billionaire Aliko Dangote’s refinery and petrochemicals business ahead of the planned listing.
